summary: Moved Link neutral special work out of the old ftLk_Init assembly file and into src/melee/ft/chara/ftLink/ftLk_SpecialN.c, adding C implementations and guarded fallback inline asm for many ftLk_SpecialN states. The PR also tightened Link-specific typing, renamed some Link projectile fields and SpecialS helpers, added SpecialN motion vars and a Vec3 group struct, and updated item/fighter headers to make those C translations compile. Evidence for intent is mostly the title and the diff; there were no review comments.
what_changed: Deleted asm/melee/ft/chara/ftLk_Init.s, a 1646-line assembly file containing ftLk_SpecialN functions, and removed its object entry from obj_files.mk.

Expanded ftLk_SpecialN.c by over 2000 lines to host Link neutral-B logic, including ftLk_SpecialN_Enter, ftLk_SpecialAirN_Enter, Start/Loop/End Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll functions, helper inlines, and guarded inline asm blocks under MUST_MATCH when WIP is not defined.
Changed ftLk_SpecialN prototypes from generic HSD_GObj* to ftLk_GObj* throughout ftLk_SpecialN.h and the implementation.
Renamed ftLk_SpecialN_UnsetFv10 to ftLk_SpecialN_UnsetArrow and replaced fp->fv.lk.x10 usage with fp->fv.lk.arrow_gobj where the field is used as Link's arrow item object.
Added Link-specific forward typedefs such as ftLk_Fighter and ftLk_GObj, and changed Link fighter vars struct naming from ftLink_FighterVars to ftLk_FighterVars.
Updated ftLk_DatAttrs field typing/naming, notably changing x4 to specialn_anim_rate and making early dat attributes floats where the SpecialN translation uses them as floats.
Added ftLk_MotionVars.specialn fields at fp+2340 through fp+2358 and ftLk_SpecialN_Vec3Group for grouped Vec3 stack/static data used by bow/arrow positioning.
Added ftLk_Init_Vec3Group as a zero-initialized const and exported it through ftLk_Init.h.
Updated item prototypes in it_27CF.h from broad HSD_GObj* to Item_GObj* for many item-object arguments, supporting typed projectile interactions.

Renamed Link SpecialS helpers to more descriptive names: ftLk_Is2071b0_5to13 to ftLk_SpecialS_Is2071b0_5to13, ftLk_Is2071b0_1to13 to ftLk_SpecialS_Is2071b0_1to13, and ftLk_800EC4B4 to ftLk_SpecialS2_Enter.
Adjusted Fighter flag bitfield access from fp->x221E_flag.bits.bN to direct aliases such as fp->x221E_b0, fp->x221E_b3, fp->x221E_b6, and fp->x221E_b7 across fighter and several character files.
Added a repository .flake8 file ignoring E501 line-too-long, likely to tolerate decomp-generated or signature-heavy lines.
smart_moves: Kept matching-safe inline asm guarded by defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP) while adding readable C alternatives, allowing progress on understanding without losing the ability to build a matching target.
Factored repeated callback assignment into setCallbacks, setting x21DC_callback_OnTakeDamage and x21E4_callback_OnDeath2 to ftLk_800EAF58 in one place.
Introduced typed aliases ftLk_GObj, ftLk_Fighter, and Item_GObj instead of continuing to pass every object as HSD_GObj*, making fighter-vs-item ownership clearer for projectile code.
Renamed the Link arrow field before translating more neutral-B logic, so later code can read as arrow_gobj rather than opaque x10.
Modeled the fp+2340 motion-var region as a Link SpecialN-specific struct, which reduces raw offset reasoning for translated animation and projectile-positioning code.
Used a small ftLk_SpecialN_Vec3Group const to mirror the original block copy/stack Vec3 behavior in C, a useful tactic for matching code that initializes multiple Vec3 temporaries from static data.
Preserved known oddities with explicit TODOs, such as questions around ftLk_Init_BoomerangExists return behavior and unused stack padding, rather than overclaiming semantics.
decomp_lessons: When a move file is too large to fully match at once, it can still be useful to relocate the assembly into the C source under MUST_MATCH and provide WIP C bodies side-by-side for incremental reverse engineering.
Projectile-heavy fighter specials benefit from early object-type separation: fighter gobj, Link fighter, and item gobj aliases prevent many ambiguous HSD_GObj* casts.
Field names should follow observed role as soon as evidence is strong enough; arrow_gobj and specialn_anim_rate make later SpecialN code much easier to review than x10 and x4.
Do not force full semantic naming when uncertain: this PR still keeps fields such as x14, x18, x8, and helper names like unkCondition/foo/bar/baz where evidence is incomplete.
For animation-command variables, an enum like cmd_unk0_bool, cmd_unk1_bool, cmd_unk2, cmd_unk3 documents indexing without pretending the command variables are fully understood.
Inline helper extraction can mirror repeated assembly patterns, but comments/TODOs should call out where shared code could be improved later, as with the air/ground enter functions.
Struct/header cleanup may be a prerequisite for matching a single fighter move because translated move logic touches dat attrs, motion vars, item constructors, callbacks, and generic fighter flags.
naming_conventions: Use character-prefixed object and fighter aliases such as ftLk_GObj and ftLk_Fighter for character-specific code.
Use Item_GObj* for item/projectile object references and item APIs when the object is known to be an item.
Prefer move-scoped names for functions once behavior is identified, e.g. ftLk_SpecialN_UnsetArrow and ftLk_SpecialS2_Enter.
Use dat attr names tied to the consuming move when known, e.g. specialn_anim_rate.
Use direct flag aliases such as x221E_b6 instead of nested bitfield access x221E_flag.bits.b6 when such aliases exist.
Keep unknown field names as xN or unk_timer when semantics remain uncertain rather than inventing gameplay names.
Motion vars are nested by character/move, as in fp->mv.lk.specialn.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved standalone .s assembly into guarded inline asm in the C file using #if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP), #pragma push/pop, nofralloc, and clang-format off comments.
Provided C versions in the #else branch for WIP/non-MUST_MATCH builds, allowing decomp work to continue while retaining exact assembly references.
Used explicit unused-stack padding under MUST_MATCH in several helpers to influence stack layout.
Used static inline helpers for repeated assembly patterns: setCallbacks, projectile spawn condition handling, Vec3 gathering, and animation-rate setup.
Represented repeated stack Vec3 initialization with ftLk_SpecialN_Vec3Group and ftLk_Init_Vec3Group.
Preserved exact float constants, including 0.0, 0.0078125, and 5 * deg_to_rad for the former 0.0872664600610733 constant.
Kept raw labels and instruction comments in inline asm, making the source searchable by original addresses such as 800ECDAC, 800ED15C, and 800EE3F0.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match ftLk_SpecialN and remove the guarded inline asm blocks.
Search for later renames of ftLk_SpecialN helper names foo, bar, baz, and unkCondition.
Check later item header cleanup around it_27CF.h Item_GObj prototype conversions.
Find later struct refinements for ftLk_DatAttrs fields x0, x8, xC, x10, and Link fv.lk.x14/x18.
Search for follow-up work on direct x221E_bN aliases and fighter flag bitfield cleanup.
